back_translation: Hiram king of Tyre sent envoys to David, along with cedar logs, stonemasons, and carpenters, to build a palace for him.
And David knew that the LORD had established him as king over Israel and had highly exalted his kingdom for the sake of His people Israel.
David took more wives in Jerusalem and had more sons and daughters.
These are the names of the children born to him in Jerusalem: Shammua, Shobab, Nathan, Solomon,
When the Philistines heard that David had been anointed king over all Israel, they all went up to seek him; but David heard of it and went out to face them.
Now the Philistines had come and raided the Valley of Rephaim.
So David inquired of God, “Shall I go up against the Philistines? Will You deliver them into my hand?” And the LORD answered, “Go up, for I will deliver them into your hand.”

So David and his men went up to Baal-perazim, where he defeated the Philistines, and he said, “God has burst out against my enemies by my hand like a bursting flood.” So that place was named Baal-perazim.
The Philistines abandoned their gods there, and David ordered them burned with fire.
Once again the Philistines raided the valley.
David inquired of God again, and God answered him, “Do not go straight up after them, but circle around behind them and attack them opposite the balsam trees.
When you hear the sound of marching in the tops of the balsam trees, go out to battle quickly, for that means God has gone out before you to strike the Philistine army.”
So David did as God commanded him, and they struck down the Philistine army from Gibeon to Gezer.
And David's fame went out into every land, and the LORD made all the nations fear him.
